eligibility: Applicant should be a student pursuing higher education.
Family income should be within Rs. 3,00,000 per year.
Applicant should belong to SC, ST, or OBC category.
benefits: Scholarship support up to Rs. 75,000 per year.
Support for tuition and maintenance expenses.
Renewable support subject to scheme rules.
application_process: Register or log in with Aadhaar-linked details.
Upload required documents and submit the form.
Track verification status on the portal.

eligibility: Applicant should belong to SC, ST, or OBC category.
Applicant should be pursuing a post-matric course.
Applicant must apply through the National Scholarship Portal.
benefits: Tuition fee reimbursement as per scheme rules.
Maintenance allowance.
Additional allowances where applicable.
application_process: Select the post-matric scholarship applicable to your category and state.
Upload documents and submit for institute verification.

eligibility: Applicant should be an eligible landholding farmer family.
Bank account and Aadhaar details are required.
Exclusion rules apply for higher-income and institutional landholders.
benefits: Rs. 6,000 per year.
Paid in three instalments of Rs. 2,000.
Direct Benefit Transfer to bank account.
application_process: Enter Aadhaar, mobile, state, and land details.
Submit the form for state verification.
Track beneficiary status on the portal.

eligibility: Eligibility is based on deprivation and occupational criteria in government databases.
Family should be listed as an eligible beneficiary.
Aadhaar or approved identity proof may be required for e-card issuance.
benefits: Health cover up to Rs. 5 lakh per family per year.
Cashless secondary and tertiary hospital treatment.
Coverage at empanelled public and private hospitals.
documents: BPL Certificate or eligible family proof
application_process: Visit the PMJAY portal or nearest Ayushman Mitra/help desk.
Use the card at an empanelled hospital.

eligibility: Applicant should not own a pucca house in their name or family member's name as per scheme rules.
Household income should fall within the eligible income category.
Other PMAY urban or rural rules apply depending on location.
benefits: Credit linked subsidy or housing assistance as per component.
Support for affordable housing.
Coverage for eligible urban and rural households.
application_process: Visit the PMAY portal or local urban/rural body.

eligibility: Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work can apply.
Applicant must seek work through the Gram Panchayat.
A job card is required.
benefits: Up to 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year.
Wage payment through bank or post office account.
Unemployment allowance may apply under rules.
application_process: Apply for a job card at the Gram Panchayat.
Submit household and adult member details.
Receive dated receipt for work demand.
Attend allotted work and track wage payment.

eligibility: Applicant should be SC, ST, or a woman entrepreneur.
Loan should be for a greenfield enterprise.
Applicant should be at least 18 years old.
benefits: Bank loan from Rs. 10 lakh to Rs. 1 crore.
Support for greenfield enterprise setup.
Handholding support through the portal.

eligibility: Applicant should run or plan a non-corporate, non-farm micro or small enterprise.
Loan should be used for income-generating business activity.
Bank appraisal rules apply.
benefits: Collateral-free loans up to Rs. 10 lakh.
Shishu, Kishore, and Tarun loan categories.
Can support working capital and business expansion.
application_process: Approach a bank, NBFC, or microfinance institution.
Select Shishu, Kishore, or Tarun category.
Receive sanctioned loan in bank account.

scheme_name: Uttar Pradesh Scholarship and Fee Reimbursement
eligibility: Applicant should be a resident of Uttar Pradesh.
Applicant should be enrolled in an eligible course.
Income and category rules apply based on the scholarship type.
benefits: Scholarship and fee reimbursement as per state rules.
Support for eligible pre-matric, post-matric, and higher education students.
application_process: Fill personal, academic, income, and bank details.
Submit and forward the hard copy to the institution if required.

eligibility: Applicant should have a severe disability as per scheme rules.
Applicant should belong to a BPL household.
Applicant should be within the eligible age range.
benefits: Monthly pension support.
Direct transfer to bank or post office account.
State top-up benefits may be available.
application_process: Apply through the state social welfare portal or local office.
Submit disability, age, BPL, and bank documents.
Track pension approval through NSAP or state portal.
